python_basics.title: Introduction to Python
python_basics.why_it_matters: Python is one of the most popular programming languages due to its simplicity and versatility. It's used in web development, data science, AI, and automation.
key_ideas: Python uses indentation to define code blocks
Everything in Python is an object

examples.title: Hello World
examples.explanation: This is the traditional first program that displays text.

examples.title: Variables
examples.code:
name = 'Alice'
age = 25
print(f'Hello, {name}! You are {age} years old.')
examples.explanation: Variables store data and can be used in expressions.

python_basics.recap: Python is a beginner-friendly language that emphasizes readability and simplicity.

variables.title: Variables and Data Types
variables.why_it_matters: Variables are fundamental to programming - they store and manipulate data throughout your program.

examples.title: Basic Variables
examples.explanation: Different data types can be assigned to variables.

examples.title: Multiple Assignment
examples.code:
a, b, c = 1, 2, 3
print(a, b, c)
examples.explanation: Multiple variables can be assigned in one line.

pitfalls: Using reserved keywords as variable names
variables.recap: Variables are essential for storing and manipulating data in Python programs.
